content:
🔥 tennis | Zig
🎯 Primary Use Case: Displaying CSV data in a formatted, readable table within the terminal.
✨ Key Features: • auto-layout to fit your terminal window • auto-themes to pick light or dark based on your terminal background • auto-format numbers • auto-detect csv vs tsv (or semis, or pipes) • titles, row numbers, border styles
📖 Summary: Tennis is a command-line interface tool written in Zig that displays CSV data in a stylish, well-formatted table directly within the terminal. It automatically adjusts to terminal size, applies color themes based on background, and formats numbers for readability.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 nerd-fonts | CSS
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide developers with programming fonts enhanced with a comprehensive set of icons and glyphs, improving the visual representation of code and terminal output.
✨ Key Features: • Patches developer-targeted fonts with a high number of glyphs (icons) • Includes glyphs from Font Awesome, Devicons, Octicons, and others • Provides a font patcher tool
📖 Summary: Nerd Fonts is a project that enhances programming fonts by patching them with a large collection of glyphs and icons from popular iconic font libraries. It provides a font patcher tool for users to customize their own fonts, offering a wide variety of installation options across different operating systems.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 PhotoGIMP | CSS
🎯 Primary Use Case: To make GIMP's user interface and controls feel familiar to Adobe Photoshop users, easing the transition for those switching to GIMP.
✨ Key Features: • Photoshop-like tool layout • Custom Splash Screen • Maximized canvas space • Photoshop keyboard shortcuts • Custom icon & name
📖 Summary: PhotoGIMP is a patch for GIMP that modifies its user interface and keyboard shortcuts to closely resemble Adobe Photoshop. It aims to provide a familiar environment for users transitioning from Photoshop to the free and open-source GIMP, enhancing usability for those accustomed to Adobe's workflow.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 zerobyte | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Automating and managing encrypted backups for self-hosted data through a web interface.
✨ Key Features: • Automated backups with encryption, compression, and retention policies powered by Restic • Flexible scheduling for automated backup jobs with fine-grained retention policies • End-to-end encryption • Multi-protocol support (NFS, SMB, WebDAV, SFTP, local directories)
📖 Summary: Zerobyte is a TypeScript-based backup automation tool designed for self-hosters. It leverages Restic to provide a web interface for scheduling, managing, and monitoring encrypted backups across various storage protocols.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 server-survival | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Teaching cloud architecture and DevOps principles through an engaging game simulation.
✨ Key Features: • Interactive 3D simulation game • Learn cloud architecture concepts • Build and scale cloud infrastructure • Handle increasing traffic loads • Defend against DDoS attacks
📖 Summary: Server Survival is an educational 3D simulation game where players act as Cloud Architects. The game teaches cloud infrastructure concepts by challenging players to build, scale, and manage a resilient system to handle traffic loads, defend against attacks, and stay within budget.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 THE-Process-Manager | Makefile
🎯 Primary Use Case: Real-time system monitoring and process management via a terminal user interface.
✨ Key Features: • Retrieve and Display Process Data • CPU and Memory Usage Calculations • Sorting • Detailed Process Information
📖 Summary: THE Process Manager is a Rust-based TUI application for real-time system monitoring. It retrieves and displays detailed CPU and memory usage for running processes, offering sorting and filtering capabilities.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
